title: Dream Finders Homes Boosts Bid for Beazer Homes to $875 Million
summary: Dream Finders Homes Inc. has boosted its offer for rival homebuilder Beazer Homes USA Inc. to $32 a share.

title: Allianz Unit to Cut as Many as 1,800 Jobs in Push to Adopt AI
summary: Allianz SE plans to eliminate hundreds of positions at its assistance and travel insurance subsidiary Allianz Partners as the company pushes forward with implementing artificial intelligence solutions.

title: Soybean Oil Hits Three-Week High as US-Iran Ceasefire Dissolves
summary: Chicago soybean oil futures climbed to a three-week high after fresh US military strikes on Iran sparked a rally in crude prices, raising the prospect of greater demand for biofuel feedstocks.

title: Wave of Earnings Surprises Will Be Hard to Repeat, Says Goldman
summary: The wave of AI-fueled earnings surprises that powered stocks in the last earning season will be tough to repeat, making it unlikely the results alone will spark a major rally, according to Christian Mueller-Glissmann.

title: Trump Says US-Iran Ceasefire Is ‘Over’ After Strikes | The Pulse 7/8/2026
summary:
"The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops.

Today's guests: Mike Bell, RBC BlueBay Market Strategy Head; Ionela Ciolan, Wilfried Martens Centre for European Studies Research Officer  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Nvidia Is World’s Most Valuable Company and Cheaper Than Hershey
summary: Nvidia is relatively cheap despite the continued domination of its chips in AI data centers.

title: Gold Drops on Rate-Hike Fears as Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Over
summary: Gold dropped as US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire with Iran has ended, stoking concerns that a renewal of fighting could again drive inflation and push up interest rates.

title: Tesco Explores Sale of Eastern European Business, FT Reports
summary: Tesco Plc is exploring a sale of its central and eastern European operations, the FT reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

title: Bangladesh Explores Debut Sovereign Bond Sale Overseas
summary: Bangladesh is studying a plan to issue its first-ever sovereign bond overseas, as the nation looks for ways to finance its record spending.

title: US Stock Futures Slide After Trump Says Iran Ceasefire Is Over
summary: US equity futures fell as investors turned risk averse after President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran has ended, fueling concerns of a renewal in fighting.

title: Emerging-Market Stocks Extend Loss as Oil Jumps on Iran Risks
summary: Emerging-market stocks extended losses as an escalation in the Middle East conflict added to the ongoing rotation out of AI beneficiaries.

title: Temasek CIO: Iran Conflict Resolution Needs More Time
summary: Temasek International's Chief Investment Officer Rohit Sipahimalani tells Bloomberg that it would take time to get to "some resolution" of the Iran conflict, noting that while both sides are interested in deescalation, the process will not be a straight line. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Stocks Fall as Trump Puts Mideast Back in Focus: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell, bond yields spiked and oil rallied after President Donald Trump thrust geopolitical risks back into focus by declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran to be over.

title: Alibaba Shares Jump Most in 10 Months as Rebound Hopes Grow
summary: Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.’s shares surged the most since September after investors turned optimistic on its earnings and shifted capital into major Chinese internet companies that had lagged the market.

title: S&P Dow Jones Warns of Potential Frontier-Market Cut for Turkey
summary: S&P Dow Jones Indices has put Turkey on a watchlist for a potential cut to frontier-market status, joining MSCI Inc. in warning of downgrades.

title: Dutch Prosecutor Summons Tata Steel Over Ijmuiden Pollution
summary: The Dutch Public Prosecution Service has summoned the local Tata Steel subsidiary on suspicion of criminal offenses including intentionally releasing harmful substances, which it said could have consequences for public health.

title: Nvidia’s $1 Trillion Slide Sends Valuation to Pre-AI Boom Levels
summary: After losing roughly $1 trillion in market value in less than two months, Nvidia Corp.’s stock is the cheapest it’s been since before the AI boom kicked off and sent the shares into the stratosphere.

title: Oil Extends Gains After Trump Says Ceasefire With Iran Is Over
summary: Oil jumped after US President Donald Trump said the tentative ceasefire with Iran is over, raising the prospect of a fresh round of conflict in one of the world’s most important energy-producing regions.

title: China Allowing Major Exports of Diesel and Gasoline This Month
summary: Chinese oil refiners have been granted more permits to export gasoline, diesel and jet fuel this month, according to people familiar with the matter, in a major relaxation of restrictions imposed during the Iran war.

title: Bonds Show Vulnerability to Iran Setback: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: German Bonds Slide as Higher Oil Prices Reignite Inflation Fears
summary: Germany’s benchmark bond yields climbed above 3% for the first time in nearly a month as a flare-up in fighting in the Middle East lifted oil prices and inflation expectations.

title: Alphabet Powers 44% Jump in India Foreign Investment, UN Says
summary: Foreign direct investment in India surged in 2025, driven largely by Alphabet Inc.’s data center project, though a decline in greenfield investments signaled a weaker investment outlook, according to the United Nations’ trade agency.

title: China’s 10-Year Bond Sale Sees Demand Gauge at Record High
summary: A gauge of demand for an auction of China’s benchmark sovereign bonds touched an all-time high on Wednesday, indicating strong appetite for the securities.

title: India Fat-Loss Drug Sales Slow After Early Spike From Generics
summary: Growth in India’s obesity- and diabetes-drug market is losing steam just months after a surge triggered by the arrival of generic semaglutide products.

title: Ugandan Farmers to Challenge $5.6 Billion Pipeline
summary: A group of Ugandan farmers will file a lawsuit in the UK High Court against the East African nation’s crude pipeline, potentially delaying exports. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Turkey’s $37 Billion Defense Firm Says Foreign Orders Doubled
summary: Turkey’s $37 billion state-run defense contractor Aselsan Elektronik Sanayi ve Ticaret AS said international orders have doubled in the past year on the back of demand fueled by the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East.

title: Japan Payment Processor Collapse Hits Banks, Restaurants
summary: The bankruptcy of Zentoshin Co., an Osaka-based credit card payment processor, threatens both the regional banks which funded it as well as the small and medium-sized restaurants that relied on its services.

title: Hedge Funds Shake Off Brutal March for Best Half Since 2021
summary: The brutal March that battered some of the world’s biggest hedge funds is already fading into the background as the firms reported their strongest first-half results in five years.

title: Private Placements Surge in Middle East on Iran War Volatility
summary: Private debt placements from the Middle East are surging as volatility due to the Iran war drives borrowers into alternative markets.

title: Thai SEC Weighs Action Against Major True Corp. Investor
summary: Thailand’s market regulator is considering legal action against an investor over inconsistencies in seven disclosure reports linked to six listed companies, including a nearly $1 billion acquisition of shares in telecom giant True Corp.
